physicalExams.subjective: F reported puppy has not eaten at all while in her care/going on 3 days now. Has frequent diarrhea, score 7, with mucus and possible blood. Pt is lethargic, has not made a sound, and sleeps through the night (uncommon for age).
physicalExams.objective: Pt is parvo negative, QAR, appears scared/anxious but tolerates being handled well and seems to have a good temperament. Gums were slightly pale, no signs of nasal or ocular discharge, respiratory distress or congestion, or skin concerns.

animals.name: Tan Sixes
animals.kennelCardMemo:
Look who’s here, it’s Tan Sixes! He’s a newcomer to APA! and we can’t wait to get to know him. When we do, we’ll share all the details with you pronto. Check back soon!

Tan Sixes has tested positive for Feline Leukemia. FeLV is not a type of cancer. It's a virus that causes a weaker immune system and often a shorter life-span. For the most part, 'FeLVies' are just like any other cat. APA! provides FeLV-related treatment and support post-adoption. Tan Sixes can only live with other FeLV+ cats, but dogs and other animals are just fine! Any questions? We'd love to talk with you about Tan Sixes’s care.

All APA! cats are fully vetted, spayed or neutered, and have updated vaccines and a microchip. Lifetime support is offered by our Cat Behavior Team.

For further information or to meet Tan Sixes, please email adopt@austinpetsalive.org.

physicalExams.subjective: Pt presented for lump in spay site.
physicalExams.objective:
BAR (very sweet kitten), mm pink/moist, CRT<2s. No oculonasal discharge. AU wnl, hair loss on outer pinnae and on head. H/L sounds wnl. Abdomen palpates soft with no pain or mass effect; 1cm x 2cm slightly fluid-y/soft swelling around spay incision site. No obvious defect in body wall palpated and no reduction of swelling can be achieved.

No fluid attained with needle aspiration.

Lateral radiograph taken.
physicalExams.assessment: Swelling around spay site -- R/O suture reaction vs open
physicalExams.plan:
Per Dr.Gordon pt got lateral right and left abdominal radiographs. Pt prescribed Meloxicam 1.5mg/mL oral suspension 0.06 mL PO (by mouth) once a day (SID) for 7 days.

Update on swelling in 7d or sooner if worsening.

memos.Disclaimer:
This dog has a Medical Consult that will be included at adoption. If you have further questions please reach out to our clinic at medicalsupport@austinpetsalive.org for help.

Tony the Tiger suffered a severe bite injury to his head that caused suspected skull fractures, infected wounds/abscesses, and loss of his left eye. Despite these serious injuries, he was alert, walking normally, and otherwise stable at his exam; he will remain permanently blind on the left side, and his longer-term prognosis depends on how well the infection and skull injuries heal. Tony is receiving doxycycline for infection, carprofen for pain and inflammation, and gabapentin for pain; medications are currently prescribed through July 31, 2026, and no refills are documented, so they should be given exactly as labeled. He is scheduled for a sedated clinic appointment on July 22, 2026, to thoroughly clean the wounds and possibly surgically close the empty left eye socket, with additional follow-up based on those findings.

Please see the medical record for the complete medical history. This is only a synopsis of the most important information.

MR 7/19/26
memos.Prior Shelter/Rescue Notes: Tony is full of life despite the injury. He’s very active, he’s 22 pounds, maybe 3-6 months old. Shepard mix

physicalExams.subjective: Pt arriving to TLAC today after poss dog fight. Missing OS.
physicalExams.plan: Per Dr. G, start gabapentin, carprofen, and doxycycline. Schedule appt at clinic for tomorrow.

physicalExams.subjective: Patient accidentally brought here this afternoon. Elected to take a quick look at patient to ensure stable. History of suspected severe dog bite wound to head resulting in abscesses and missing left eye +/- skull fractures. Not started on any medications.
physicalExams.objective: BAR (shy but sweet puppy, becomes a little mouthy/playful once he warms up), mm pink/moist, CRT<2s. No ocular discharge OD. Skull above OS appears to be caved in with the globe of OS missing and the orbits conjunctiva showing. Various punctures with purulent discharge slightly oozing above right eye region. No abnormal movements around the nasal cavity regions while breathing. No obvious misalignment of jaw, did not get a good look inside mouth though (understandably head shy). AU mild to moderate waxy debris, more sensitivity when looking at AD (though just tilting head away, no pineal-pedal reflex). H/L sounds wnl. Abdomen palpates soft with no pain or mass effect. Ambulatory x4. Intact male with both testes descended.
physicalExams.assessment: Skull fracture + abscesses + missing OS suspect secondary to previous large dog bite wounds.
physicalExams.plan:
Rx: Doxycycline 10mg/kg PO BID x14.
Rx: Carprofen 4.4mg/kg PO SID x14d.
Rx: Gabapentin 10mg/kg PO BID x14d.

Scheduled for a sedated appointment on Wednesday 7/22 for sedated cleaning +/- closure of OS socket.

physicalExams.subjective:
Pt presents for lethargy, decreased appetite, and ocular discharge OD.
Pt is a new intake from flooding. Foster reports no v/d/c/s. Pt sleeps all day. Parvo snap test performed at TLAC this morning, negative.
physicalExams.objective:
Lethargic to dull mentation, mm light pink/moist, CRT<3s. Mild clear ocular discharge OU, OS eye lids have mild swelling but no severe chemosis or conjunctivitis. No nasal discharge. Patient is holding tongue slightly out and appears to have diffuse moderate swelling of the mandible bilaterally. No obvious fractures or areas of instability palpated, though mild discomfort noted when jaw was fully opened. Firm slightly mobile soft tissue swelling on the left lateral jaw region around the area of the parotid lymph node/parotid gland. Submandibular LNs palpate around 1cm diameter, all other peripheral LNs palpate wnl. H/L sounds wnl. Abdomen palpates soft with no pain or mass effect.

Radiographs taken -- No obvious fractures, though circular opacity seen within the L parotid region, but difficult to tell what it is associated with. Pt very wiggly for radiographs.
physicalExams.assessment: Facial swelling, FUO -- R/O vaccine reaction vs insect envenomation vs infectious vs puppy strangles vs open
physicalExams.plan:
Administered 30ml LRS SQ with 0.14ml each Ondansetron/Famotidine/Cerenia, 0.25ml Vitamin B12, and 0.16ml Reglan.
Administered Diphenhydramine and Dexamethasone-SP IM. No improvement in facial swelling after 15 minutes, but slight improvement in mentation.

Rx Benadryl 25mg 0.25T PO BID x7d.
Rx Prednisone 5mg 0.5T PO SID x3d to start tomorrow PM, then 0.25T PO SID x3d, then 0.25T EOD x2 doses.
Rx AmoxiClav 45.7mg/ml 0.45ml PO BID x7d,
Rx Gabapentin 100mg/ml 0.2ml PO BID x7d.

Foster to update if no improvement, if facial swelling worsens, if patient appears to have trouble breathing, or if pt overall seems to decline.

memos.Volunteer Notes:
How long was your Sleepover
9 days

Select at least three traits that best describe this dog:
Affectionate
Velcro Dog
Silly
Fun-loving
Cheerful

What made you smile or laugh about them?
Everything! This dog is pure joy. He is goofy and clumsy, will do anything to get close to you including tumbling over himself before curling up in the crook of your arm. His underbite causes his bottom teeth to stick out, which gives him the funniest expressions, and when he listens intently his right ear perks straight up while his left ear stays folded. There is nothing cuter! He recently felt confident and comfortable enough to show off some zoomies in our backyard, and that was pure joy to watch!

Is there anything a future adopter should know to set them up for success?
Limo has the sweetest soul and aims to please! He has been so tolerant of baths, car rides, crate training (primarily at night, he's learning to be brave in there alone during the day), sharing toys, and getting on potty break schedules. That said, he's still young and is learning how to be a proper gentleman! It will take no time at all to teach him basic commands and how to be a good citizen on a leash. He can still be a little bit nervous on walks but is growing more confident by the minute as he settles in. He will do everything in his power to be a good boy! He is phenomenal at going potty out in our backyard, but gets distracted on walks - so, if you're wanting to make sure that he has gone potty before heading out of the house or letting him roam free, I would recommend taking him to a yard or designated grass patch that he gets used to when getting started.

Where did the dog sleep
Crate

How was the dog in the car?
Some whining or pacing, but settled

How was the dog when left alone?
Some whining or pacing

What is their housetraining status?
Mostly housetrained - Understands the concept and thrives on a routine, but may have occasional accidents if left alone for too long, during transitions, or when excited. May have 1-2 accidents per week.

How did the dog do in the bath?
Had fun and loved it!

How did they do with other adults in the home or visitors? Did they meet visitors, or only hang with you?
Loved everyone

How did they do with cats or small animals (if present)?
Not applicable

How did they do with children (if present)?
No children present

How did they do with other dogs (if present)?
Loved them

If you select 'Not Recommended" to any of the answers above. Please use this space to tell us why you choice that selection.

How would you describe their energy level?
Moderate

How were they on leash?
Pulled sometimes

Do you want to add any details about the information you provided above?

Would you adopt this dog if you could? Why or why not?
I would adopt this dog in a heartbeat if I could! I will be heartbroken to say goodbye to him, but will be so excited for his future family to hit the jackpot with him! He is a joy to be around and makes me laugh all the time. He has shown unconditional love every second that we have had him, and is the best cuddler I've ever met! He doesn't bark, is so gentle and friendly with dogs (he has been very respectful of our small resident dogs, who are 6 and 13) and people. He is honestly a dream!

memos.Sleepover notes:
"How long was your Sleepover
One night so far

Select at least three traits that best describe this dog:
Affectionate
Velcro Dog
Trusting

What made you smile or laugh about them?
Jo Lee is so sweet. She’s loves to cuddle up against someone any chance she can.

Is there anything a future adopter should know to set them up for success?
She’s a delight! But she’s got some puppy tendencies to grow out of, like: she likes to chew and she’s not quite potty trained. She slept in a crate all night but she whined for about 20 minutes before settling in. And she’s a classic Velcro dog - never wants to leave your side.

Where did the dog sleep
Crate

How was the dog in the car?
Relaxed
Anxious and restless

How was the dog when left alone?
Some whining or pacing

What is their housetraining status?
In progress / working on it: Needs very frequent potty breaks and close supervision. Has multiple accidents per day, but goes outside once in a while.

How did the dog do in the bath?
Tolerant

How did they do with other adults in the home or visitors? Did they meet visitors, or only hang with you?
Loved everyone

How did they do with cats or small animals (if present)?
Not applicable

How did they do with children (if present)?
No children present

How did they do with other dogs (if present)?
Loved them

How would you describe their energy level?
Moderate

How were they on leash?
Pulled sometimes

Would you adopt this dog if you could? Why or why not?
We’re definitely thinking about it! But we’ve only had her since yesterday afternoon."

I understand that if refuse to surrender the Animal to APA!, as required herein, APA! may outcome the animal as “Lost/Stolen” and/or pursue any and all available legal remedies against me.

animals.name: Eggplant Parmesan
animals.kennelCardMemo:
Toasty, Tender, Chin-Scratchable

Eggplant Parmesan is a beautiful brown girl with warm orange undertones and the tiniest white toes - not mittens, just toes! She’s highly affectionate and always ready for loving, though she has plenty to keep her busy.

The secret to getting this active girl to sit still is a good chin scratch. Add some head scratches right between her ears, and Eggplant Parmesan will happily pause everything to soak up the attention.

All APA! cats are fully vetted, spayed or neutered, and have updated vaccines and a microchip. Lifetime support is offered by our Cat Behavior Team.

For further information or to meet Eggplant Parmesan, please email adopt@austinpetsalive.org

Ongoing, I'm fostering

Select at least three traits that best describe this dog:
Chill
Low-maintenance
Polite

She gives kisses

Is there anything a future adopter should know to set them up for success?
She needs a calm, low-energy home. A senior wanting a companion animal would be perfect. I would not place her with kids or an active or noisy environment.

Where did the dog sleep
Your Bed

How was the dog in the car?
Relaxed

How was the dog when left alone?
Relaxed

What is their housetraining status?
Fully housetrained - Consistently holds it when left alone, and gives clear signals when they need to go outside. May have had accidents in the first couple days, but leveled out completely once the dog learned the routines.

How did the dog do in the bath?
Tolerant

Loved everyone

Not applicable

No children present

Loved them

Low

Not taken on a walk


Would you adopt this dog if you could? Why or why not?

animals.name: Shadow Horse
animals.kennelCardMemo:
Meet a handsome 3-year-old German Shepherd who brings together everything people love about the breed: intelligence, loyalty, playful energy, and a huge heart. He enjoys meeting new people and is always ready to make a new friend, especially when cuddles might be involved.

In his foster home, he has been fully house trained and gets along well with other dogs. He is also remarkably quiet—his foster has yet to hear him bark! He settles nicely into a home routine and has shown that he can be an easy, enjoyable companion.

This playful guy loves long walks and would be thrilled to join someone who enjoys exploring the neighborhood or heading out on daily adventures. Exercise helps him burn off his youthful energy, and once he has had a good walk, he is perfectly content to relax and recharge.

One of his favorite pastimes is lounging by a window and watching the world go by. Whether he is observing people, cars, or neighborhood activity, he is happy to take it all in from his favorite viewing spot before settling in for a nap.

Most of all, he is a complete cuddle fanatic. He does not simply sit beside you—he leans his whole body in, wraps his paws around you like a hug, and gazes up with the sweetest, most loving expression. His goofy, enthusiastic way of showing affection is impossible to resist, and he is ready to give one lucky family all the hugs they can handle.

He's fully house trained, gets along well with other dogs, and enjoys meeting new people. He's very quiet—we have yet to hear him bark.

He has a playful, energetic side and loves long walks, which help him burn off energy. Once he's had his exercise, he's content to spend time lounging. He's loves watching the world go by from his favorite windows.

He is an absolute cuddle fanatic. He doesn't just sit next to you—he leans in, wraps his paws around you like a hug, and gazes at you with a loving expression. So cute and goofy about showing affection.

First night we had him he insisted on cuddling on the couch.

Large, strong athletic dog that is still probably growing. Needs active family. Also has a significant prey drive on walks so need to be careful with smaller animals.

Crate

Relaxed

Relaxed

Fully housetrained - Consistently holds it when left alone, and gives clear signals when they need to go outside. May have had accidents in the first couple days, but leveled out completely once the dog learned the routines.

Seemed nervous or anxious

Loved everyone

Not recommended

No children present

Warmed up over time

Serious prey drive on walks. I wouldn't have him around smaller animals.

High

Pulled constantly

Yes we might! He gets along with our dog, loves all humans, and is very sweet.

memos.Disclaimer:
The dog you are adopting or fostering has been rescued from one of our more under-resourced partner shelters and unfortunately has come to us with ticks on them. Rest assured that upon their arrival they were treated with Nexgard which will kill fleas within hours and the ticks within 24-48 hours. Often the dead tick will detach on its own, but not always and might require removal.

We understand that this can be concerning to bring into your home, but we have some ideas that have worked for other fosters and adopters previously. In order to limit the spreading of the ticks while they are dying we recommend keeping the dog in a spare bathroom, bedroom or a x-pen area for this initial period. This will help with clean-up if they are in a smaller space where it is easier to clean up afterwards. Additionally, baths can help with removing the ticks! Just be aware that after the first bath you’ll want to use water only so you aren’t stripping your dog’s coat of their naturally occurring oils which could make them itchy.

If you have any concerns about your resident dog getting ticks from your foster or adopted dog, please let us know and we can provide a single dose of tick meds for them as well while your new APA! dog is getting rid of their ticks.
